channel.title: Cooklang Guides and Tutorials: From Zero to Working Recipes on Cooklang
channel.description: Recent content in Cooklang Guides and Tutorials: From Zero to Working Recipes on Cooklang

item.title: Cooklang Editor Setup: VS Code, Vim, Obsidian, and More
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Cooklang files are plain text, so any editor works. But syntax highlighting and LSP support make the experience much better. Here's how to set up your editor of choice.

item.title: Build Custom Recipe Reports with CookCLI and Templates
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: CookCLI has a template system that turns .cook files into shopping lists with store links, cost breakdowns, aisle-organized lists, and CSV exports. This post walks through the cook report command, the ingredient database, and real working templates.

item.title: Building with Cooklang: A Parser Integration Guide
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: You want structured recipe data — ingredients with quantities, steps with inline references, timers, cookware. Here's how to parse Cooklang in your language of choice, with quick-start examples in TypeScript and Python, and a map of the full ecosystem.

item.title: Plain Text Recipes: A Beginner's Guide to the Cooklang Format
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: A step-by-step introduction to writing recipes in Cooklang — the plain text format that turns your recipes into structured data. Convert your first recipe in five minutes.

item.title: Publish Your Recipe Collection as a Static Site with `cook build web`
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: CookCLI can now turn your recipe folder into a self-contained static website — host it on GitHub Pages, Netlify, an S3 bucket, or just open the HTML file directly.

item.title: Markdown Recipe Template: Free Templates & a Better Alternative
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Copy-paste Markdown recipe templates that work in any text editor, Obsidian, or static site — plus an honest look at where Markdown falls short and what Cooklang adds.

item.title: Migrating Your Recipes to Cooklang (From Any App or Format)
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Stuck in a recipe app you can't escape? Here's how to migrate your recipes to plain .cook files from websites, Paprika, Mealie, photos, and Markdown — without doing it all at once.

item.title: Publishing Your Recipe Collection as a Website
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Your .cook files are already structured data. Here's how to turn them into a real website — static, fast, free to host, and readable by Google's recipe search.

item.title: The Complete CookCLI Guide: From Install to Daily Use
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: A hands-on walkthrough of CookCLI covering installation, parsing recipes, generating shopping lists, scaling servings, and every command you'll actually use.

item.title: Try Cooklang in Your Browser: Playground Walkthrough
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: The fastest way to try Cooklang requires nothing but a browser tab. This walkthrough shows you exactly what to expect when you open the playground.

item.title: Cooklang for Obsidian: Manage Recipes in Your Vault (2026 Guide)
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: A step-by-step guide to using the Cooklang plugin for Obsidian. Turn your vault into a recipe manager with syntax highlighting, interactive timers, shopping lists, and a beautiful preview mode.

item.title: Recipe Format: How to Write, Structure & Store Recipes Properly
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Recipes are scattered across screenshots, apps, bookmarks, and handwritten cards — all in incompatible formats. Here's what makes a good recipe format, how the existing options compare, and why Cooklang solves the problem that plain text and JSON-LD can't.
